> _Verbatim report from the deob-compile-and-net-depth workflow (2026-06-01)._

## INCOMING PACKET FIDELITY AUDIT — "World & Map State" opcode group

Scope: the server→client opcodes that mutate world/scene state — region load, scenery add/remove, boundary (wall/door) add/remove, ground-item add/remove, the bulk region cull, and projectiles. Ground truth = the jar's clean Vineflower decompile (`decompiled/normalized-clean/client.java`, obf `client.b(int,byte,int)` = `handlePacket`, plus `client.a(int,int,boolean)` = `loadRegion`). Oracles = OpenRSC `Payload235Generator` and LeadingBot `mudclient.java` (rev ~235) / mudclient204. Deob under audit = `src/client/Mudclient.java`.

Opcode-number reality check (jar vs OpenRSC Payload235): the jar's numbers match Payload235 — SCENERY=48, BOUNDARY=91… **except the jar's actual handler bodies bind BOUNDARY to opcode 99 and GROUND_ITEM to opcode 91**, i.e. the jar's `handlePacket` has `if(99==var1)`→boundary (touches `Ah/Zf/Ni/Gj/Le`) and `if(var1==91)`→ground-item (touches `hf/rd[]/Jd/yk/Hj/Ng`). Payload235 lists BOUNDARY=91 / GROUND_ITEM=99, the reverse. The deob faithfully reproduces the jar's binding (99=boundary, 91=ground-item), so the deob is correct against ground truth; only the inline doc note at `Mudclient.java:4063-4065` carries the OpenRSC-numbering labels. Scenery opcode 48 is encoded in the clean jar as `if(-49==~var1)` (i.e. `var1==48`), which is why a plain `==48` grep misses it.

---

### Opcode 191 — SEND_PLAYER_COORDS (region origin → triggers region/zone load)
`Mudclient.java:4110-4137`. Reads `i(-2231)` align, `f(-106,11)` regionX (`Lf`), `f(-106,13)` regionY (`sh`), `f(-82,4)` local anim, then `loadRegion(sh,Lf,false)`; subtracts scene origin, `worldX/Y = local*Ug+64`, snaps camera on region change, recreates local player via `d(...)`. Clean `client.java:14220-14258` identical (bit widths, order, `+64`, the `Ug` multiply). **MATCH.**

### loadRegion — region/zone load (obf `a(int,int,boolean)`, clean L11537 bytecode-only)
`Mudclient.java:6066-6199`. rk-fail guard, cache-hit window test (`yj==bc && Jg<z<Rk && Fi<x<Ne`), 48-tile chunk snap (`(z+24)/48`, `Qg=chunkZ*48-48`, `Ne/Rk/Fi/zg/Jg`), `k.a(z,-90,x,yj)` world.setOrigin, then three rebase loops. Verified against the clean bytecode: loop 1 bounds on `eh` touching `Se/ye/vc/hg/bg` (scenery); loop 2 bounds on `hf` touching `Jd/yk/Ng/Hj/rd` (ground items, calls buildEntityModel); loop 3 bounds on `Ah` touching `Zf/Ni` (boundaries). Array roles, bounds, and chunk math all **MATCH**. The deob's *inline comments* mislabel loop 1 "wall/object", loop 2 "door/diagonal", loop 3 "ground-item spots" — comment-only. **MINOR** (comment mislabel).

### Opcode 48 — SEND_SCENERY_HANDLER (scenery/object add+remove)
Clean `client.java:14510-14659` (`if(-49==~var1)`): per-entry — if marker≠255: `f(255)` objType, `h()` x, `h()` y; compact-out any scenery at (x,y) removing matched model (`Ek.a(hg,-1)`, `Hh.a(vc,Se,ye,4081)`); add gate `if(objType!=60000)` builds the model (`kh[fb.f[type]].b(-2)`, dims from `ub.g[]`/`f.f[]`, orientation from `Hh.b(x,y)`, special-case type 74), stores into `Se/ye/vc/bg/hg[eh++]`. Marker==255 path culls scenery at the anchor tile. Confirmed by LeadingBot `mudclient.java:1537-1607` (opcode 27, `l8 != 60000`). **The deob `handlePacket` has NO opcode-48 branch and no scenery-add code path** (`grep` of every `opcode==N` shows 48 absent; `eh`/`Se`/`ye`/`vc`/`hg[]` are only ever read or reset/compacted, never appended). **DIVERGENCE — entire handler missing.**

### Opcode 91 — ground-item add+remove (jar binding; OpenRSC calls this BOUNDARY)
`Mudclient.java:4380-4442` vs clean `client.java:14901-15027`. Marker==255 removal-run: deob reads `h()`,`h()` anchors (no spurious un-read) and compacts (`rd[].rb=idx+10000`), scene-removing culled items — **MATCH** clean. Add/remove-single path: reads `f(255)` itemId, `h()` x, `h()` y, `h()` dir — read order/widths **MATCH** (clean stores dir as `byte var102`; deob as `int dir` — sign-extension identical for small dirs, **MINOR**). **BUT the add gate diverges:** deob uses a `placed` boolean (set on (x,y,dir) match) and adds `if(!placed)`; the clean jar adds `if(itemId!=65535)` (`var187=65535; var197=var50; if(var187!=var197)`), confirmed by LeadingBot `mudclient.java:1967` (`if(l9 != 65535)`). Consequences: (a) a re-sent item at an occupied tile (real id, match found) → clean removes-old-then-re-adds (item stays, model rebuilt), deob removes and skips re-add (**item disappears**); (b) a pure-remove sentinel id 65535 on an empty tile → clean adds nothing, deob `placed==false` so it **adds a phantom item with id 65535** (bogus model / likely `buildEntityModel` fault). **DIVERGENCE.**

### Opcode 99 — boundary/wall (door-open) add+remove (jar binding; OpenRSC calls this GROUND_ITEM)
`Mudclient.java:4185-4247` vs clean `client.java:14370-14507`. Add/remove-single path **MATCH**: `f(255)` dir/type (bit15=remove), `h()` x, `h()` y; `(dir&0x8000)` → single-remove by (x,y,dir); else add at `Ah` inheriting lighting from co-tile scenery `Se/ye/vc` (`Le[Ah]=h.b[vc[s]]`). The deob's `if/else` (remove vs add) is equivalent to the clean's `var55=-123` sentinel. **However the removal-run (marker==255) path has a 1-byte buffer DIVERGENCE:** deob `4187-4191` does `if(mg.a()==255){ mg.w--; anchorX=Lf+mg.h()>>3; anchorY=sh+mg.h()>>3; }` — the spurious `mg.w--` un-reads the 255 marker, so `h()` then reads the 0xFF marker byte itself as anchorX (→ `Lf+(-1)>>3`, wrong tile culled) and the real anchorX byte as anchorY, leaving the real anchorY byte unconsumed → the boundary stream desyncs by 1 byte for every subsequent entry in the packet. The clean (`14385` then `14471-14473`) reads the marker and does **not** un-read it before the two anchor `h()` reads (3 bytes consumed: marker+anchorX+anchorY); mudclient204 (`pdata[l+1],pdata[l+2]; l+=3`) agrees. Note the deob's own ground-item-91 removal-run (line 4382, no `w--`) is correct, making the 99 `w--` an internal inconsistency / transcription bug. **DIVERGENCE.**

### Opcode 211 — SEND_REMOVE_WORLD_ENTITY (bulk re-cull of walls + scenery + ground items)
`Mudclient.java:4590-4654` vs clean `client.java:15327-15466`. `count=(length-1)/4`; per anchor reads `a(false)` = **signed short** (2 bytes) for X and Y (correctly wider than the byte-coded 48/91/99 streams), `>>3`. Walls: keep if tile-delta≠(0,0), compact (no scene-remove — matches clean's wall loop). Scenery: compact kept (`hg[].rb=idx`), else `Ek.a(hg,-1)`+`Hh.a(vc,Se,ye,4081)`. Ground items: if delta==(0,0) remove (`Ek.a`+`Hh.a`) else keep (`rb=idx+10000`). All bounds (`Ah`,`eh`,`hf`), read widths, and remove/keep predicates **MATCH** (deob's `rx==0&&ry==0` == clean's `0==var149 && -1==~var164`). **MATCH.**

### Projectiles — opcode 234 sub-types 3 & 4 (within SEND_UPDATE_PLAYERS)
`Mudclient.java:4302-4310` (type 3) and `4342-4350` (type 4) vs clean `client.java:14755-14763` / `14849-14856`. Both sub-types read `f(255)` sprite then `f(255)` target (unsigned shorts). Type 3 sets `h=target, w=nc, z=-1, a=sprite`; type 4 sets `w=nc, h=-1, z=target, a=sprite`. Read widths, order, and field assignments **MATCH** the clean base exactly. (The per-tick projectile-position interpolation at `Mudclient.java:7194-7208` consumes `player.w/h/z` consistently; not a packet read.) **MATCH.**

---

## NUMBERS

- **Opcodes audited (this group): 7** — 191 (region origin/load trigger), loadRegion (region/zone load), 48 (scenery add/remove), 91 (ground-item add/remove), 99 (boundary/door add/remove), 211 (bulk world-entity cull), 234 projectile sub-types 3 & 4.
- **MATCH: 4** — 191, 211, 234-projectiles, and the 99 add/remove-single path (core boundary logic).
- **MINOR: 2** — loadRegion (rebase-loop comment mislabels; code correct) ; opcode-91 ground-item dir stored as `int` vs clean `byte` (sign-extension equivalent for real dir values).
- **DIVERGENCE: 3** (semantic, all world-perception-affecting):
  - **[CRITICAL] Opcode 48 SEND_SCENERY_HANDLER — entire handler MISSING** in the deob `handlePacket`. No scenery add/remove path exists; `eh/Se/ye/vc/hg[]/bg` are never populated from the live region stream, so trees/signs/furniture/door-objects and their lighting/orientation never appear or update mid-session, and the 99/211 handlers that read `eh` operate on stale scenery. Clean: `client.java:14510-14659`; oracle LeadingBot `mudclient.java:1537-1607` (`!=60000` add gate).
  - **[HIGH] Opcode 91 ground-item add gate WRONG** — deob adds `if(!placed)` (placed = match found) instead of the jar's `if(itemId!=65535)`. Re-sent items at occupied tiles silently vanish; a 65535 remove-sentinel on an empty tile spawns a phantom item. `Mudclient.java:4413/4427/4433` vs clean `client.java:15009-15022`; oracle LeadingBot `:1967`.
  - **[HIGH] Opcode 99 boundary removal-run 1-byte desync** — spurious `this.mg.w--` at `Mudclient.java:4188` re-reads the 0xFF marker as anchorX, mis-culls walls (`Lf+(-1)>>3`) and shifts the buffer cursor by 1 byte for the remainder of the boundary packet (cascading corruption of all later wall/door add/removes in that packet). Clean `client.java:14385/14471-14473` does NOT un-read the marker; the deob's own opcode-91 removal-run (`:4382`, no `w--`) is the correct pattern.
